title: quassia undulata
intro: Quassia undulata, also known as the palo de amarillo, is a plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Rutaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of quassin, a lignan compound with anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of quassia undulata include its ability to treat parasitic worms, diarrhea, and fever, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antibacterial agent. Quassia undulata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a treatment for various ailments.
